note: Flags/allegiances the generator draws from. `kind:nation` are sovereign flags; `kind:company` are chartered-monopoly flags that fly under a parent nation. `color` is an sRGB hex used later for allegiance tinting. `rivals` drives wartime hostility (cross-checked against wars.json). `article`/`navalPrefix` feed the naming construction rules in names.json.

powers.name: Great Britain
powers.note: Dominant naval and mercantile power across the era.

powers.name: France
powers.note: Britain's chief rival; Atlantic colonial and slaving trade.

powers.name: Dutch Republic
powers.note: Fades after 1795 (Batavian Republic / French client).

powers.name: Kingdom of the Netherlands
powers.note: Succeeds the Dutch Republic (which ends 1795). The Japan trade 1817-1858 ran on the Netherlands Indies colonial government's account from Batavia in chartered private hulls - NOT the NHM, which took over only in 1859 (verified 2026-07-18).

powers.name: Spain
powers.note: Carrera de Indias; frequently allied to France.

powers.name: Portugal
powers.note: Brazil & Estado da India; usually British-aligned.

powers.name: Sweden
powers.note: Neutral carrier; Baltic iron/timber and SOIC China trade.

powers.name: Denmark-Norway
powers.note: Neutral carrier; Danish Asiatic Co.

powers.name: United States
powers.note: Post-1783 only; neutral-trade carrier and China trade.

powers.name: East India Company
powers.note: British monopoly on the India & China trade.

powers.name: V.O.C. (Dutch East India Co.)
powers.note: Dissolved 1799; Batavia & Dejima trade.

powers.name: Swedish East India Co.
powers.note: Gothenburg-Canton tea trade.

powers.name: Danish Asiatic Co.
powers.note: Tranquebar & Canton trade under Danish neutral flag.

powers.name: Russia
powers.note: Diversity layer (PLAN-2 §5): Arkhangelsk furs & naval stores; the Russian-American Co. after 1799. Spawns once its ports are baked (Phase B).

powers.name: Ottoman Empire
powers.note: Diversity layer: Smyrna's Levant trade, Kaffa, Ottoman Yemen (Mocha). Carriage largely Greek- and Armenian-crewed under the Ottoman flag.

powers.name: Omani Empire
powers.note: Diversity layer: Muscat's dhow trade; took the Swahili coast from Portugal (Mombasa 1698).

powers.name: Safavid Persia
powers.note: Diversity layer: Bandar Abbas silk & cotton after Hormuz falls (1622); fades with the Safavid collapse.

powers.name: Ryukyu Kingdom
powers.note: Diversity layer: Naha's licensed tribute trade — the tiny kingdom that laundered the China–Japan exchange.

powers.name: Sultanate of Gowa
powers.note: Diversity layer: Makassar's free-trade spice smuggling against the VOC monopoly, until the city falls in 1669.

powers.name: Kingdom of Siam
powers.note: Diversity layer: Ayutthaya's crown junks in the Japan and China trade, until the city's destruction in 1767.

powers.name: Republic of Ragusa
powers.note: Diversity layer: the neutral Adriatic carrier — 'argosy' is her word — under Ottoman protection.

powers.name: Duchy of Courland
powers.note: Diversity layer: Duke Jacob's improbable colonial fleet out of Windau (Tobago, the Gambia).

powers.name: Brandenburg-Prussia
powers.note: Diversity layer: the Brandenburg African Company's brief Gold Coast venture out of Emden.

powers.name: The Hanse towns
powers.note: S2 backbone flag: Hamburg, Danzig, Riga — the Hanseatic and post-Hanseatic German-Baltic carriers. Riga's sovereignty changes (Swedish 1621, Russian 1710) are smeared under this flag, noted.

powers.name: Republic of Venice
powers.note: S2 backbone flag: the Serenissima, to its fall in 1797.

powers.name: Republic of Genoa
powers.note: S2 backbone flag: banker and carrier of Habsburg silver.

powers.name: Tuscany (Livorno)
powers.note: S2 backbone flag: the free port's own small marine; most Livorno traffic flies other flags, as it did. Extended to 1850 with the era: the Grand Duchy of Tuscany lasted until 1859, and Livorno's free port went on trading through the Napoleonic interruption and the 1815 restoration.

powers.name: Kingdom of Naples
powers.note: S2 backbone flag: the Mezzogiorno's grain and consumption trade.

powers.name: Mughal India (Gujarati marine)
powers.note: S2 flag: Surat's own merchant fleet — India's ships in India's trade (Das Gupta). Fades with Mughal authority after 1700; ends as a spawning flag c.1760.

powers.name: The Nanyang junk trade
powers.note: S2 flag (user-funded): the private Fujian/Canton junk diaspora — distinct from the Qing state (a non-spawning shore power). THE Euro-centrism corrective made sailable.

powers.name: Tsushima (Sō domain)
powers.note: S2 flag (user-funded): the licensed carrier of the Korea trade — ready for Busan's promotion (tranche 2). Era extended to 1850: the 1815 end was the old chart horizon. The So domain held its Korea trade to the Meiji restoration, and the Maldive sultanate ran to 1953.

powers.name: The Bugis network
powers.note: S2 flag: the archipelago's own carriers — Makassar under Gowa, then the diaspora after 1669 (Reid). Carries the asserted bugis-carrying system.

powers.name: Golconda (Qutb Shahi)
powers.note: S2 flag bundle: Masulipatnam's sovereign — non-spawning shore power, ready for the port's promotion (tranche 2).

powers.name: Ming China
powers.note: Phase-4 flag bundle: China's sovereign before the Qing conquest (Beijing fell 1644; the south held to the 1660s). Non-spawning shore power — the flag over Canton/Amoy/Shanghai etc. in the early era, where qing is anachronistic. The Chinese junk trade itself sails under china-junk-trade throughout.

powers.name: Japan (Tokugawa)
powers.note: Phase-4 flag bundle: the shogunate under sakoku — the sovereign of Nagasaki and the host of the Dutch factory at Dejima (which flies dutch, being Dutch-run). Non-spawning shore power.

powers.name: Sultanate of Patani
powers.note: S2 flag bundle: the queens of Patani — non-spawning shore power, ready for the port's promotion (tranche 3).

powers.name: W.I.C. (Dutch West India Co.)
powers.note: Diversity layer: the Atlantic counterpart of the VOC — Brazil, Elmina, St Eustatius, and the treasure-fleet war.

powers.name: Russian-American Company
powers.note: Diversity layer: Sitka sea-otter furs for the Canton market.

powers.name: African polities (embarkation)
powers.note: Not a spawning flag — the coastal polities (Hueda, later Dahomey) that controlled embarkation at Whydah. Present so the African side of the Atlantic trade is named, not effaced.

powers.name: Efik city-states
powers.note: Diversity layer, not a spawning flag: Old Calabar's merchant houses (Duke Town, Creek Town), who controlled the Bight of Biafra trade on their own terms.

powers.name: Joseon Korea
powers.note: Diversity layer, not a spawning flag: Busan's Japan House (Waegwan) — trade conducted by licensed Tsushima vessels. Era extended to 1850 with the chart: Joseon lasted to 1897 and the Qing to 1912 — the 1815 end was the old horizon, not history.

powers.name: Zamorin of Calicut
powers.note: Diversity layer, not a spawning flag: the Malabar pepper sovereign who resisted the Portuguese monopoly for two centuries.

powers.name: Maldive Sultanate
powers.note: Diversity layer, not a spawning flag: Malé's cowrie-money fishery, carried to Bengal in local and Indian bottoms. Era extended to 1850: the 1815 end was the old chart horizon. The So domain held its Korea trade to the Meiji restoration, and the Maldive sultanate ran to 1953.

powers.name: Crimean Khanate
powers.note: Diversity layer, not a spawning flag: Kaffa's Black Sea trade — including its slave market, to be treated with the same sober factual framing as the Atlantic trade.

powers.name: Nguyễn lords (Đàng Trong)
powers.note: Diversity layer, not a spawning flag: Hội An's licensed junk fair — silk, ceramics, aloeswood.

powers.name: Qing China
powers.note: Not a spawning flag — controls Canton, where Western company ships were confined to the Thirteen Factories. Era extended to 1850 with the chart: Joseon lasted to 1897 and the Qing to 1912 — the 1815 end was the old horizon, not history.

powers.name: Regency of Algiers
powers.note: The Regency of Algiers, conventionally dated from 1516 (NOT 'Ottoman from 1525'). Ends 1830 at the French conquest; the port itself continues under france from that year via eraPowers.

powers.name: Regency of Tunis
powers.note: The Regency of Tunis: Ottoman from 1574, Husainid from 1705, still a Regency past 1850.

powers.name: Regency of Tripoli
powers.note: The Regency of Tripoli: Ottoman from 1551, Karamanli 1711-1835, then Ottoman again. The maritime terminus of a Saharan caravan trade.

powers.name: Mowachaht (Nuu-chah-nulth)
powers.note: Nootka Sound was Mowachaht territory, and Maquinna's people controlled who traded there - the American and British fur ships anchored at their sufferance, not in a vacant harbour. A SHORE power (it spawns no vessels, like african and qing): the ships on the nootka-fur lanes fly American and British colours, which is the honest division. Flagging the anchorage itself American would have written the owners out of their own harbour.

powers.name: Peru
powers.note: Independent from 1821; Callao fell in January 1826, the last Spanish position in South America.

powers.name: Chile
powers.note: Independent from 1818. Valparaiso became the first Pacific call for shipping round the Horn and a rising entrepot through the 1830s and 40s.

powers.name: Austrian Netherlands (Imperial)
powers.note: The double-headed eagle at sea: private Ostend voyages from 1715, the chartered Ostend Company 1722-27, wound up by the Treaty of Vienna, with a Bengal afterlife at Banquibazar to 1744. Deliberately clamped to that span.

powers.name: Sultanate of Aceh
powers.note: The pepper sultanate at Sumatra's northern tip, shipping direct to the Red Sea and outflanking the Portuguese Cape route. Its own polity and its own shipping — it was filed under the Zamorin here by mistake, which put an Acehnese fleet under a Malabar flag.

powers.name: Sultanate of Banten
powers.note: Banten: the archipelago's pepper emporium and Batavia's rival until the VOC conquest of 1682-84. Its own sultanate, not Gowa's — the two are a thousand miles and a different people apart.

powers.name: Hudson's Bay Company
powers.note: Chartered 1670. One or two ships a year to the Bay and home again inside the ice-free window — a tiny, utterly regular traffic.
